item.title: RBC: Client Advisor, Winter 2025 Student
item.description:
locations
VANCOUVER, British Columbia, Canada
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 27, 2024 (9 days left to apply)
job requisition id
R-0000103157
Job Summary
Participates in and supports the operational activities of a business unit. Applies experience and complete working knowledge of the job and policies/practices to perform a combination of routine tasks and varied administrative/operational assignments.
Job Description
Are you 2-3 years away from graduation and plan to pursue a career in banking? We are looking for a Student Intern at our Mount Pleasant branch!
In order to be eligible for this 4-month Student position, you must either:
Be returning back to school after the work term ends
If you are not returning back to school (i.e., are graduating in Dec. 2024), you must require the work term as a mandatory component to in order to graduate successfully.
What is the opportunity?
Open the door to the first of many opportunities at RBC and experience the challenges and rewards of working in the financial services industry. If you have the drive to deliver exceptional customer service, you can build a great career with us and gain progressive learning opportunities year over year through our Internship Program.
You will gain foundational skills as a Client Advisor, the face of RBC. You work on the front lines, serving clients, carrying out transactions, solving problems and spotting sales opportunities. This is an important role providing advice to clients and finding solutions that meet their individual needs.
What will you do?
Serve clients by delivering exceptional customer service through completing transactions, engaging in needs-based conversations, and communicating with clients throughout the branch
Identify needs and provide tailored financial advice and solutions to clients, when possible, at the point of first contact
Contribute to team sales results by listening and spotting opportunities to offer additional advice, introduce clients to the capability of RBC partners, or personally fulfil client solutions
Understand & resolve client concerns, where possible, at first point of contact
Educate & assist clients with using RBC digital capabilities
Manage risks by adhering to compliance routines, processes, and controls to protect client and shareholder interests while completing transactions
What do you need to succeed?
Must-have
Proven customer service skills- Drive and self-motivation, as well as excellent communication skills and emotional intelligence- Digital literacy across a broad range of devices (i.e., smartphones, tablets, laptops, etc.)
Personal flexibility to work a variety of hours including evenings and weekends- Eagerness to learn and determination to succeed
What’s in it for you?
We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.
Excellent career development and the potential to return each summer for progressive opportunities until graduation
A world-class training program in financial services
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Job Skills
Communication, Computer Literacy, Detail-Oriented, Interpersonal Relationships, Listening Effectively, Personal Development, Personal Initiative, Teamwork
Additional Job Details
Address:
505 BROADWAY W:VANCOUVER
City:
VANCOUVER
Country:
Canada
Work hours/week:
37.5
Employment Type:
Full time
Platform:
PERSONAL & COMMERCIAL BANKING
Job Type:
Student/Coop (Fixed Term)
Pay Type:
Salaried
Posted Date:
2024-10-17
Application Deadline:
2024-10-27
Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Employment
At RBC, we embrace diversity and inclusion for innovation and growth. We are committed to building inclusive teams and an equitable workplace for our employees to bring their true selves to work. We are taking actions to tackle issues of inequity and systemic bias to support our diverse talent, clients and communities.

We also strive to provide an accessible candidate experience for our prospective employees with different abilities. Please let us know if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process.
Join our Talent Community

Stay in-the-know about great career opportunities at RBC. Sign up and get customized info on our latest jobs, career tips and Recruitment events that matter to you.

Expand your limits and create a new future together at RBC. Find out how we use our passion and drive to enhance the well-being of our clients and communities at jobs.rbc.com.

item.title: CIBC: Associate, Commercial Banking
item.description:
locations
Yellowknife, NT
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 1, 2024 (14 days left to apply)
job requisition id
2415925
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What you’ll be doing
You will join CIBC’s Commercial Banking team to provide support to account teams through client acquisition, relationship management and credit management processes as well as providing transactional support for client specific financing solutions. This role will prepare you for a rewarding career as a Relationship Manager, Commercial Banking and strategic partner to some of Canada’s leading companies. You will be committed to delivering on flexible business solutions, dedicated business expertise and timely business advice to help clients realize their goals.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. To successfully perform the work, you’ll be on-site full-time.
How you’ll succeed
Business development support - Assist Relationship Managers, Commercial Banking in coordinating activities through the sales cycle and liaising with other units of CIBC as necessary. Assist with the execution of business development initiatives and contribute to the achievement of growth targets by identifying business opportunities and developing financing solutions to meet the needs of the client.
Research & evaluation - Assist in evaluating business growth opportunities through comprehensive industry, client and prospect research. Prepare analyses in support of credit submissions including structuring and documentation.
Client experience - Proactively assess your clients’ needs and propose solutions to meet those needs. Interact with assigned clients to facilitate prompt responses to questions, enquiries or issues in order to provide “best in class” service. Apply acquired industry/company knowledge to the development of client/prospect proposals for financial and strategic solutions to meet client needs.
Who you are
You can demonstrate experience in financial statements and accounting principles including valuation techniques and cash flow analysis.
You have a degree/diploma in Business, Finance, Accounting, or related field.
You give meaning to data. You enjoy investigating complex problems and making sense of information. You communicate detailed information in a meaningful way.
You act like an owner. You thrive when you're empowered to take the lead, go above and beyond, and deliver results.
You embrace and champion change. You'll continuously evolve your thinking and the way you work in order to deliver your best.
You're driven by collective success. You know that collaboration can transform a good idea into a great one. You understand the power of an inclusive team that enjoys working together to bring a shared vision to life.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability.
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential. We aspire to give you a career, rather than just a paycheck.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive salary, incentive pay, banking benefits, a benefits program*, defined benefit pension plan*, an employee share purchase plan, a vacation offering, wellbeing support, and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
We cultivate a culture where you can express your ambition through initiatives like Purpose Day; a paid day off dedicated for you to use to invest in your growth and development.
*Subject to plan and program terms and conditions
What you need to know
CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit.
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
Job Location
Yellowknife
Employment Type
Regular
Weekly Hours
37.5
Skills
Analytical Thinking, Business Banking, Business Development, Client Service, Financial Advising, Portfolio Management, Researching, Results-Oriented

item.title: Manulife: Financial Sales Associate
item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 25, 2024 (6 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100341
The opportunity
The Financial Sales Associate is responsible for providing life insurance solutions to Bank's clients. He/she will work in coordination with branch sales and service teams, thus we are looking for a dynamic individual, who has an independent mindset and the drive to succeed.
Responsibilities
Work closely with branch sales and service teams to provide innovative product solutions to address client's protection, and long-term saving needs
Strengthen the long-term relationships with clients by providing excellent customer service
Perform other tasks as required.
How will you create impact?
The role will directly report to an Area Manager and will work closely with branch sales and service teams
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.
What we are looking for
Candidate must possess at least a Bachelor's/ College Degree
Work experience in bancassurance sales is highly preferred for this position but those with sales and customer service experience gained from banking, transport, pharmaceutical, FMCG, hospitality and IT industries are also encouraged to apply
Must be self-driven and highly motivated
Amiable, with good interpersonal and communication skills
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.
Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
In Office

item.title: Manulife: Director, Web Domain Strategy & Optimization
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Waterloo, Ontario
Montreal, Quebec
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 18, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100415
We pride ourselves on our innovative approach and commitment to excellence. As we continue to grow, we are seeking a strategic and analytical Director of Web Domain Strategy and Optimization to join our team and drive our online presence to new heights. The Director, Web Domain Strategy & Optimization. Reporting to the AVP, Digital Experience Enablement, they will be responsible for developing and executing comprehensive strategies to optimize the company's public web presence and improving digital experiences, including enhancing website performance, managing web content, and aligning the digital strategy with business goals.
This role requires a leader with a deep understanding of web technologies and content management platforms, web analytics, user experience, SEO, and digital marketing. The successful candidate will work closely with cross-functional Marketing and Business unit teams to streamline our web presence, optimize website performance, manage content, support strategies that drive traffic, improve conversion rates, and ensure a seamless user experience
Key Responsibilities:
Strategic Planning:
Define and implement the overall web platform strategy aligned with business objectives, customer needs, and digital trends to drive engagement, lead generation, and customer satisfaction and work with stakeholders to ensure web footprint is scalable and maintainable.
Identify opportunities for growth and improvement in web presence and performance.
Stay current with industry trends, best practices, and emerging technologies.
Content Management Services:
Oversee the development and maintenance of a content management system (CMS) that supports efficient, scalable content creation and updates.
Ensure content is relevant across our web presence, high-quality, and aligned with brand standards, while managing workflows for content approval and publishing.
Web Optimization:
Continuously improve website performance through SEO, A/B testing, mobile responsiveness, speed optimization, and user experience enhancements.
Work with stakeholders to Implement data-driven strategies to increase conversion rates, reduce bounce rates, and improve site traffic.
Collaborate with content creators to ensure that web content is optimized for search engines and user engagement.
Web Channel Transformation Leadership:
Champion digital initiatives that improve the user experience, streamline processes, and enhance digital touchpoints across all web properties.
Cross-Functional Collaboration:
Work closely with stakeholders in Marketing, IT, Product, and other departments to ensure the website supports broader business initiatives, campaigns, and customer engagement strategies.
Work closely with the digital marketing teams to ensure cohesive and integrated online campaigns.
Partner with IT stakeholders to ensure seamless integration of the website with internal systems (CRM, CMS, analytics tools) and third-party technologies to improve the overall digital experience.
Team Leadership:
Manage team who work on complex digital projects within a fast-paced, matrixed environment.
Lead, mentor, and manage a team of web publishers, developers, content managers, and experimentation and optimization team members to execute digital projects efficiently.
Compliance and Security:
Ensure the website adheres to industry regulations, security standards, and best practices in data management, accessibility and applicable privacy laws
Stakeholder Engagement:
Present recommendations, progress, and performance reports to senior leadership and key stakeholders, providing data-driven insights and recommendations.
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, information technology, or a related field.
Proven experience (8+ years) in web strategy, optimization, and digital marketing or a similar role. Within the insurance, financial services, or regulated industries would be an asset
Strong understanding of content management systems (CMS), web technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript), web analytics tools (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, etc.), SEO, and best practices in web performance optimization.
Experience with Adobe Experience Manager
Excellent leadership and team management skills.
Strong project management abilities with a track record of delivering successful web initiatives
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to convey complex digital strategies to non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to develop and execute a long-term vision for the company’s web presence while being hands-on with day-to-day operations.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.
Why Join Us:
Opportunity to make a significant impact on the company’s web presence and growth.
Collaborative and innovative work environment.
Competitive salary and benefits package.
Professional development and growth opportunities.
Values-first culture; We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together
Can you do this? Come and join the winning team at Manulife, where you’ll be expected to lead, inspire, create superb work and help deliver on our bold mission.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Primary Location
Toronto, Ontario
Working Arrangement
Hybrid
Salary range is expected to be between
$103,050.00 CAD - $185,490.00 CAD
If you are applying for this role outside of the primary location, please contact recruitment@manulife.com for the salary range for your location. The actual salary will vary depending on local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience, and education/training. Employees also have the opportunity to participate in incentive programs and earn incentive compensation tied to business and individual performance.
Manulife offers eligible employees a wide array of customizable benefits, including health, dental, mental health, vision, short- and long-term disability, life and AD&D insurance coverage, adoption/surrogacy and wellness benefits, and employee/family assistance plans. We also offer eligible employees various retirement savings plans (including pension and a global share ownership plan with employer matching contributions) and financial education and counseling resources. Our generous paid time off program in Canada includes holidays, vacation, personal, and sick days, and we offer the full range of statutory leaves of absence. If you are applying for this role in the U.S., please contact recruitment@manulife.com for more information about U.S.-specific paid time off provisions.

item.title: Manulife: Life Underwriter
item.description:
locations
CAN, Ontario - Full Time Remote
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 30, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24070602
Manulife’s underwriting team is renowned for thought leadership and innovative ways to underwrite complex files. We are looking for growth-minded, service-oriented underwriters to join our team!
We offer industry-leading training and development and support our underwriting team with development opportunities within and beyond traditional underwriting roles. As a member of our exceptional Underwriting team, you'll be part of shaping the future of insurance excellence.
This is a work from home position and we are open to candidates in any Canadian province.
Position Responsibilities:
Conduct risk assessment and classification by Manulife's philosophy and standards.
Communicate and collaborate with sales and head office colleagues to provide the highest quality and competitive decisions to our customers.
Maintain positive and effective relationships with internal and external partners.
Consistently deliver the best customer experience by making every interaction significant.
Align with consumer laws and regulations regarding risk classification, confidentiality and disclosure.
Identify and propose solutions to problems and procedures related to the underwriter's work.
Required Qualifications:
0-3 years of underwriting experience.
$0 - $2,000,000+ Life signing authority.
Sales and relationship-focused mindset with a drive for earning business and continuing to earn business in this competitive market.
Working towards, or completion of FALU, FLMI and ACS designations.
Strong knowledge of medical terminology including physiology, anatomy, pharmacology, imaging, diagnostic testing and treatments.
Strong knowledge in how non-medical risks impact mortality, including hazardous occupations, sports, avocations and lifestyle.
Experience with financial reports and training in anti-money laundering practices.
Excellent oral and written communication skills.
Preferred Qualifications:
Bilingual (French & English) an asset – If in Québec, the successful candidate must communicate in English and French to support clients from various jurisdictions outside of Quebec.
Bilingual English and Mandarin, an asset.
University or college degree in a related discipline such as medical, research or financial.
Strong negotiation skills.
Interest in representing the company via external presentations, panels, conferences, etc.
Experience in co-signing, referral reviews, mentoring, etc.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.

item.title: Manulife: Solution Implementation Director
item.description:
locations
Boston, Massachusetts
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 31, 2024 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100153
Market Solutions at John Hancock is responsible for rapidly bringing new solutions to market and scaling them to meet business objectives, while anticipating market needs and guiding product development. The team positions solutions to highlight their value, competitive differentiation, and market fit, working across all functions to ensure readiness for a fast paced brokerage market. The Solution Implementation Director coordinates the implementation of new solutions, ensuring launch readiness through collaboration with Marketing, Sales & Distribution, Product, Operations and Technology, and leads post-launch actions to ensure market success. As part of Market Solutions, you will play an integral role working across functions and capabilities to ensure new solutions meet market demands.
Position Responsibilities:
Develop a comprehensive go-to market plan for a new solution, which includes market opportunity, product fit, and marketing / distribution strategies.
Lead end-to-end delivery of for a new solution, through close collaboration with key collaborators across the business in Product, Marketing, Sales & Distribution, Operations and Technology.
Ensure Marketing and Sales & Distribution readiness and that messages are clearly understood, all vital marketing collateral is developed, and training is complete so that internal and external partners are sufficiently ready at launch.
Be responsible for the backlog of post-launch actions to ensure market acceptance, working across the business to ensure agreed-upon delivery and timely resolution of any issues or challenges.
Report regularly on project status, including key achievements, risks, and challenges – taking proactive measures to remove obstacles to success.
Required Qualifications:
5+ years of experience project/program management in delivering major cross-functional Insurance and/or Annuity solutions.
Experience in Life Insurance brokerage and or Annuity market highly desirable.
Deep understanding and experience with project management tools and methodologies along with Agile experience.
Preferred Qualifications:
Extensive experience working with senior management stakeholders, building trust, and communicating effectively.
Strong relationship, communication, and influence skill.
Experience in handling stakeholders and driving impact.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: Manager Business Support
item.description:
locations
Montreal, Quebec
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 9, 2024 (22 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24071087
The Opportunity
This position is responsible to lead the QA Fund Operations team in the Global Wealth and Asset Management (GWAM) Canada Operations. Please note that this is not a traditional IT support, business analyst or project management role.
GWAM Canada Operations team is seeking a Manager, Business Support, who will be responsible to support the Fund Operations Admin team by implementing VPA system enhancements, and other business-related projects.
Responsibilities
Leading an engaged team through coaching, recognition, recruitment, and development, while working on your own tasks and initiatives.
Providing leadership by handling projects and initiatives through the agile working model, while ensuring they are delivered within the agreed upon timeline.
Handle deliverables, development, and team capacity.
Communicating effectively and regularly to a varied audience of direct reports, peers, and senior management on issues, changes, delays.
Proactively interacting and communicating with collaborators and build strong relationships with partners.
Establish and adhere to process related to risk & controls, data governance and system development life cycle (SDLC).
Demonstrating your analytical and critical thinking skills to ask questions, understand our business drivers and needs, and bring order to a fast-paced environment where priorities change frequently.
Using your own technical skills to work on projects or collaborate with the team to gain efficiencies.
Communicating project status updates to management, colleagues and peers.
How will you create impact?

Reporting to the Director, Fund Operations, the successful candidate will be a highly motivated, influential, accountable, and a passionate leader who has a confirmed leadership track record in building engaged teams, strong peer relationships and facilitating the resolution to problems while supporting collaborators, systems, and processes in a fast-paced and changing environment.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
University degree in Finance, Accounting, Data Analytics or Computer Science with a minimum of (7-10) years of relevant experience.
Enjoy supporting other teams/peers with problems and helping to bring groups together to address issues and facilitate resolution.
Excellent relationship and collaborator management skills – build trust.
Consider operational risks and ensure effective controls are in place for your team.
Can clearly articulate business needs between technical and non-technical resources as well as present strategy and vision to senior management
Resilient, adaptable to change and lead others through change.
Quick leaner that picks up new ideas, concepts, technologies, and tools easily
Dedicated to continuous improvement.
Knowledge and application of Microsoft Office suite
Effectively consider downstream and collaborator impacts and can coach your team to do the same.
Shown ability to collaborate and negotiate with various team members with competing priorities.
Comfortable driving solution progress forward – even when your team doesn’t have all the answers yet.
Assets
Knowledge of the fund life cycle and fund operations
Related experience with leading teams that are in a supportive role to help enable teams, peers, collaborators to deliver and run the business.
Strong knowledge of financial services operations and/or systems
Bilingualism, written and spoken.
Knowledge of VPA and Trust Accounting.
Bilingual (French & English) is required - The successful candidate will be required to communicate in English and French to support clients from various jurisdictions outside of Quebec.
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture: We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity: We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation: We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion: We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship: We build a business that benefits all collaborators and has a positive social and environmental impact.

item.title: Manulife: Mortgage Administrator
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 2, 2024 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100860
The opportunity
Manulife's commercial mortgage portfolio is comprised of assets domiciled in both the US and Canada and have both internal and external investors. We have direct interactions with the borrowers, solicitors and title companies, as well as both internal and external stakeholders. Within Closing/Post Closing we have a Legal Services Team which primarily is responsible for preparing all documentation for the registration of mortgage discharges after Canadian and US loans have been paid in full while ensuring we maintain accurate security on the current portfolio.
Responsibilities
Review of Commercial mortgage files (all legal documentation)
Review and knowledge of recorded documentation (title search)
Prepare all discharge documentation for Canada and US; which includes ensuring the correct parties are executing the document and ensuring proper notarization rules are followed.
Maintain and renew existing PPSA and UCC filings
Assist in researching previously paid out mortgages throughout Canada and the U.S. and prepare discharges when required for outside Lawyers or previous Mortgage customers.
Mortgage file management, completing checklists, updating mortgage systems, scanning documentation.
Manage and maintain physical and digital archives, ensuring proper organization and retrieval of documents and records.
How will you create impact?
This position works within a mortgage servicing area working closing with other teams for our US and Canadian clients on a wide variety of transactions and initiatives. Our ideal candidate is organized, professional, responsible and committed to helping us serve our internal clients as well as external. Create, review and revise documents required to complete legal filings in accordance with federal and state regulations.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.

You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
What we are looking for
Law Clerk program / Legal Administration diploma
3 – 5 years in similar role specializing in Commercial Real Estate / Mortgage or Residential Real Estate is desirable
Good knowledge of business/mortgage/real estate legal terminology
Knowledge of MS Office; Outlook and other business and legal research tools
Proven proficiency in e- registry systems (Teranet)
Comfortable to work in a team environment, with ability to work independently;
Ability to identify risks; and ability to handle sensitive matters with professionalism
Ability to coordinate with several stakeholders both internal and external (borrowers, solicitors, title companies)
Contribute to continuous improvements of processes within team

item.title: Manulife: Internal Sales Consultant
item.description:
locations
Boston, Massachusetts
Atlanta, Georgia
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 2, 2024 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24091840
The Internal Sales Consultant works as a member of a team composed of a wholesaler and relationship manager who are responsible for educating financial advisors on retirement plans, particularly 401(k), and working with them to add John Hancock’s retirement solutions to their respective product offerings. The ISC works to understand the needs and offer solutions that are aligned with the company key decision makers, the financial advisor and John Hancock.
Responsibilities
Establish and maintain relationships with Financial Advisors and Registered Investment Advisors.
Partner with a Regional Vice President in the field to create, develop and implement programs that drive opportunities in their territory.
Leverage Investment Tools (RPAG, FI360, John Hancock Investment Comparison Tool) and provide insight for registered representatives helping them improve portfolio construction.
Educate advisors on the retirement industry. Discuss the differences and advantages of products to help determine what solution best suits their client’s needs. (SIMPLEs, 401(k), Individual IRA’s, Cash Balance plans, DB plans, non-qualified, etc).
Become well versed in JH’s product offers as well as other providers to assist advisors in determining the product that best meets their client’s need. A central part of this is assisting with plan design.
Assist advisors and TPAs with sold case paperwork and the onboarding of plans
Recommend and strategize with TPA Partners to develop unique and optimal Plan Design for each Plan Sponsor/401k Plan.
Support the advisor’s business growth by helping them leverage John Hancock’s story and services to gain competitive advantage, as well as, providing industry insights.
Establish and execute a business strategy and annual territory plan with the team to achieve revenue and growth targets by: Segmentation of advisors, leveraging partners (DCIO/TPA) to find additional opportunities, and identifying innovative approaches to help current advisors grow their book of business.
Provide service and support by contacting advisor audience by soliciting/prioritizing opportunities and analyzing needs (pricing, service issues, features) when existing plans go out for RFP
Review investment line-up from current 401(k) provider, analyze and compare to the JH platform to ensure the plan sponsor has appropriate and diverse fund line-up offering providing full style box coverage.
Lead projects for department initiatives to fully implement new processes, strategies, and more.
Qualify new advisor prospects by gaining insight on their business model and composition of current clients
Participate in training and coaching development programs to enhance skills and provide opportunities for advancement within the ISC role and other firm wide sales, marketing, and management related functions
Adhere to the highest levels of integrity and professional conduct
How will you create impact?
Working in conjunction with a Regional Vice President, this individual will proactively manage and cultivate their territory primarily via telephone, email and web activity. Using discretion and judgement, they will strategically manage their territory providing insight, practice management ideas and marketing support to registered representatives in their defined territory.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
What we are looking for
Bachelor's Degree or equivalent work experience
A minimum of 2-3 years progressive work experience, preferably in phone and/or sales type roles.
A commitment to attain the Securities Industry Essentials, Series 6, 63 plus state insurance and variable licenses within 4 months of hire
Passion for growing a territory with a positive mindset
Excellent relationship building, communication, presentation and negotiation skills
Superior business acumen especially with retirement plan products and intermediary strategies
Versatility to use multiple tools and systems to efficiently research and prepare competitive proposals for advisors
Honed analytical and problem-solving skills particularly in relation to providing guidance and managing a territory
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.

Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on

time left to apply

The Opportunity
As a Senior Data Science Engineer, you will take a leading role in designing and delivering AI/Advanced Analytics solution in cloud-based production environments to business; be accountable for delivering business value with data by focusing on key corporate objectives, including portfolio optimization, expense efficiency, growth acceleration, digital customer leadership, fraud detection, and high-performance of the team. The ideal candidate would have experience in implementing Analytics solutions in finance, health and/or investment organizations.
Responsibilities
Domain expert to leadership for technical guidance around data and AI/ML solution design and standard methodologies
Design, develop and deploy of AI/ML solutions in cloud environment
Integrate AI/ML solutions with enterprise applications and business processes
Responsible for MLOps, DevOps toolchain and Continuous Development, Continuous Integration and Automated Testing using Jenkins
Provide guidance on analytics concepts for broader advanced analytics group, and inspire adoption of advanced analytics, data engineering and data science across organization
Present findings, share insights, and make recommendations that impact sales; and communicate results in written, oral and presentation formats
Innovate ways to source and use data to support modeling efforts applying structured and unstructured data; and big data technologies
Work with multi-functional distributed team members to identify and prioritize practical, high-impact insights across a variety of core business areas

You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.

Advanced degree in a quantitative field (Computer Science, Information system, Mathematic, Statistics, Economy, Finance, etc.)
Advanced experience with PowerBI, Salesforce, MLFlow, GitHub (ideally with co-pilot)
Familiar with distributed computing frameworks (e.g. Spark), cloud platforms (e.g. AWS, AzureML, Databricks), containerization, and analytics libraries (e.g. pandas, NumPy, matplotlib)
Experienced with developing APIs or web application development
Strong sense of ownership and superb problem-solving capability

Strong business acumen and finance knowledge, experienced in wealth asset management/ finance

item.description:

Full time

Manulife’s Wellness, Disability and Life Team is looking for Canadian Armed Forces Disability Case Managers for our Halifax office (however the role is flexible and remote, or hybrid work is optional)
As a Case Manager for the CAF LTD plan, you will be required to support our plan members across Canada through illness, recovery and return to work by providing them with disability benefits and proactive case management focused on return to work. The role involves the ability to apply critical judgment when determining whether to issue disability payments, strong analytical skills when reading contractual wording and medical information, prioritizing and actioning tasks daily and a considerable amount of telephonic communication. This role is specific to the opportunity of working with military Veterans who have served our country, and is often rewarding work.
Responsibilities
Proactively manage a dedicated caseload in compliance with specific service level agreements and targeted turnaround times.
Actioning daily administrative tasks that would include responding to emails and telephone calls within 24 hours, monitoring tasks associated with caseload and sending out correspondence
Assessing claims based on contractual, medical, and vocational barriers
Developing strong relationships and frequent collaboration with plan sponsors, plan members, treatment providers and internal partners (ie disability specialists, rehabilitation specialist and medical consultants) to drive cases to successful return to work or job ready resolution
Writing letters to communicate pertinent benefit related information based on analytical reasoning
Demonstrating resiliency, empathy and compassion when listening and communicating with plan members regarding potentially sensitive physical and mental health information that may involve delivering difficult claims related decisions
What we are looking for
A bachelor’s degree in a healthcare program or equivalent
The successful candidate will be required to communicate in English and French in order to support clients from various jurisdictions.
Background in a health-related field would be an asset
The ability to make difficult decisions within tight time constraints
Disability management and/or Group Benefit knowledge, an asset
At ease with both incoming and outgoing calls
Ability to work well as both a team member and an independent self-starter
Strong organization and prioritization skills required to manage different types of tasks
Excellent written and oral communication skills in both French and English
Computer skills would be an asset (MS Office, typing skills, transcription skills etc)
Awareness and general knowledge of Canadian Armed Forces would be an asset
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.
Our commitment to you
Values-first culture -We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity -We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation -We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion -We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship -We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.

item.title: Manulife: Enrollment Specialist
item.description:
locations
Boston, Massachusetts
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 2, 2024 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100883
The Enrollment Specialist works on a broader team responsible for educating and helping our participants at a critical decision point when deciding whether to join their employer’s 401(k) with John Hancock 401(k).
The successful Enrollment Specialist is well versed in John Hancock 401(k) products and their underlying asset classes and investment options. Using discretion and judgement, the Enrollment Specialist assesses participants’ unique retirement goals, educates them on the specifics of their John Hancock 401(k) plan, and then offers solutions that are aligned with participants’ best interests. This involves establishing individual retirement goals, setting a deferral rate, thoroughly explaining the plan’s investment options and 401(k) plan specifics and details. While assessing the participants retirement and investment acumen, the enrollment specialist utilizes a suite of John Hancock educational tools to assist the participant in developing a customized strategy aimed to reach their individual goals, including the assisting and guiding participants to make investment selections that are in line with their specific goals. Enrollment Specialists also identify account consolidations opportunities during the enrollment process, and collaborate Consolidation Specialists to facilitate related asset gathering activities.
Position Responsibilities:
Member of inbound/outbound phone-based team focused on educating and help participants at a critical decision point when determining whether to join their employers 401(k) plan
Establish and maintain relationships with participants throughout the enrollment process
Develop a customized retirement savings plan for the participant based on an individual’s needs
Discover if the individual has prior retirement savings that may be available to combine into their new 401(k); collaborate with Consolidation Specialists to extend the service proposition
Educate participants on the rules and regulations of their specific plan and how to best utilize it to meet their retirement needs
Become well versed in JH’s product offerings as well as all retirement products (SIMPLEs, 401(k), Individual IRAs, Cash Balance plans, DB plans, non-qualified, etc.) and current laws.
Assist participants, advisors and relationship managers with all options available to enroll participants into their plan
Recommend a deferral amount based on information attained when consulting with the participant based on their individual goals and situation
Educate participants on all the investment options available to them in the plan
Deliver superior service in a professional manner while always putting the participant first
Effectively explain investment strategies and discuss the plan investment line-up in order to help the participant properly invest based on their individual preferences
Participate in training and coaching development programs to enhance skills and provide opportunities for advancement within PFS and other firm wide sales, marketing, and management related functions
Adhere to the highest levels of integrity and professional conduct
Meet monthly production and activity targets
Required Qualifications:
Fluency in both Spanish and English is required
Bachelor's Degree with a preference on Business and or Finance or equivalent years' experience
A commitment to attain the Series 6 and 63 plus state insurance and variable licenses within 6 months of hire
Passion for assisting participants on a one-on-one basis
Versatility to use multiple tools and systems to efficiently research and ensure it is in the participants best interest as well as within the plans specification
Preferred Qualifications:
An excellent understanding of retirement plans, retirement plan marketplace, and retirement vehicles
Thorough understanding of John Hancock story, products, services and procedures
Strong understanding of investments and portfolio analysis
Strong understanding of regulatory environment and its impact on business and the plan documents
Ability to anticipate, meet and/or exceed customer needs, wants and expectations
Ability to quickly recover from adversity
Ability to effectively communicate, build rapport and relate well to all kinds of people
Ability to manage time and multiple task to achieve desired outcomes
Ability to affect other’s actions, decisions, opinions or thinking
Ability to hold oneself accountable for personal actions
Exhibit high levels of integrity and professional conduct
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
#LI-JH

The Opportunity:
John Hancock Investment Management (“JHIM”), a division of Manulife Financial, is a premier provider of asset management and investment strategies. John Hancock has been helping individuals and institutions build and protect wealth for 159 years. At a time when trust and confidence are at a premium, we are proud to offer a full range of investment strategies that carry one of America's strongest and most recognized corporate brands.
JHIM is looking for an External Wholesaler to engage new and existing clients to drive sales across the entire suite of JHIM investment products. This position is responsible for coverage of financial advisors across multiple channel. This territory offers significant growth potential for the right candidate.
Responsibilities
Distribution of Mutual Funds, ETFs, 529 plans, UCITS, SMAs, DCIO and Private Placement offerings.
Maintain and build client relationships by providing timely information on products, capital market intelligence, practice management advice and above all, exceptional client service.
Conduct a robust schedule of client-facing activities through group and one-on-one in-person meetings, virtual meetings, phone calls, and emails.
Initiate new business through prospecting.
Represent John Hancock Investment Management at in-person and virtual events including national and regional conferences, seminars, and sales training meetings.
Develop strong relationships with internal business partners, including assigned Internal Wholesaler and other home office stakeholders.
Effectively manage the assigned territory by properly leveraging all available resources including but not limited to product specialists, portfolio consultants and prudent allocation of travel & entertainment budget.
Recommended Experience
Bachelors Degree preferred
Minimum of 3-5 years of asset management wholesaling experience required
Series 7 and 66 or 63 licenses required
CFA or CIMA designation preferred
Must have excellent communication and presentation skills – both in-person and virtual
Must have effective time management, technological, and organizational skills
It is preferred that candidates already be established in the local market

item.description:

time type
Full time
posted on

JR24101067
Associate Director, HNW Wealth Planning Consultant (Agency Channel)
The customer is the focus of everything we do, and millions of end users rely on our products daily. We believe in the value of empowering our professionals with the resources to solve critical problems for the future of our business, which is why we need you.
Position Responsibilities:
Develop and execute a sales strategy to generate new business opportunities within the high-net-worth market segment through agency channel
Partner with experienced agents to create insurance opportunities by identifying customers wealth management needs and introduce appropriate insurance solutions on their high-net-worth portfolios
Provide support to agents through coaching on HNW Legacy Planning solution including products knowledge of Trust, Will and Power of Attorney Writing, etc.
Launch campaigns and other activities to boost sales activities and no. of active agents, including but not limited to organizing seminars, forum, district-based training/activity
Ensure strict adherence to internal compliance/governance guidelines and market regulations.
Stay up-to-date with industry trends, market developments, and regulatory requirements.
Required Qualifications:
University degree or above
Over 8 years related HNW business experience focusing on private client solution, wealth planning solution, or trust solution.
Experience in using Insurance and Fiduciary structure (e.g offshore trust) as planning tools
To help our channel stakeholders (e.g. agency force) understand the benefits of legacy planning structures and in the administrative and technical aspects of these structures
To work into building good relationship with professional intermediates in the private wealth sector, e.g. Trust specialist, accountants and lawyers
Familiar with HNW insurance market landscape, especially in the Mainland Chinese Market.
To drive and execute initiatives from firmwide perspective in the HNW/Private Client segment
Good understanding of the international development impacting HNWIs
Private banking Wealth Planner, Tax Advisory, or Trustee experience preferred
Result orientated with emphasis on customer and quality
Good analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong interpersonal and communication skills as well as presentation skills (visual and verbal)
Significant level of risk, sales quality, and compliance awareness
Fluent in Chinese, Putonghua, and English
Professional insurance qualifications and licenses is an advantage
Candidate with less experience will consider as Senior Manager

item.title: Manulife: Cloud IT Asset Management Analyst, GTSM
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 15, 2024 (28 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24100689
Join our Global Technology Services and Support Team!
We are looking for a candidate with a combination of direct hands-on industry Cloud and ITAM experience. A background that combines technical and business aspects of hardware, software, end-to-end cloud management, and technical currency, and FinOps. The candidate will be passionate, persistent, and detail oriented with an ability to clearly articulate an objective view of the IT asset inventory, associated risks, and opportunities to enable business decision making.
This role is key to Manulife’s digital transformation, including evolving, maturing, and automating processes that manage our IT assets. The successful candidate will report directly to the Manager, IT Asset Management.
Responsibilities:
Develop and implement asset management strategies, policies, and procedures for cloud-based IT assets.
Track and manage the lifecycle of cloud assets, including procurement, deployment, maintenance, and decommissioning.
Maintain an accurate inventory of all cloud assets, including software licenses, subscriptions, and virtual machines.
Monitor and analyze cloud usage and spending to identify cost-saving opportunities.
Collaborate with unit leaders on cost optimization strategies, including rightsizing resources and identifying unused or underutilized assets.
Provide regular reports on cloud spending and usage trends to senior management.
Conduct regular audits of cloud assets to verify compliance and identify any discrepancies.
Develop and maintain documentation related to cloud asset management processes and policies.
Work closely with IT, finance, procurement, and other groups to ensure alignment of cloud asset management activities with organizational goals.
Use of cloud management platforms, asset management tools, and other technologies to automate and streamline asset management processes.
Stay up-to-date with emerging trends and technologies in cloud asset management and recommend improvements to existing processes and tools.
Qualifications:
A minimum of 5 years + of experience in IT asset management, with at least 2 years focused on cloud asset management.
Experience with major cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and cloud management tools.
Solid understanding of cloud computing concepts, technologies, and standard processes.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret complex data and make informed decisions.
Proficiency in asset management software and cloud management platforms.
Certifications (Preferred but not required):
IT Asset Management (ITAM) Certification (e.g., Certified IT Asset Manager - CITAM)
Cloud certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, Google Cloud Professional Cloud Architect)
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.

item.title: Manulife: Manager, Risk Management
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 16, 2024 (29 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24060719
The Opportunity

As the Manager, Risk Management you will be part of the Vendor Information Risk Management team and conduct assessments on information security controls. In this role, you will measure the effectiveness of the security controls, identify control gaps, and provide business advice and guidance on the various IT risks and/or contract gaps associated with the supplier.
This is an individual contributor position based out of the Toronto or Waterloo office with a hybrid work arrangement (3 days in office).

Responsibilities
Conduct information security risk assessments for new and existing vendors. Reviews include IT Controls, Business Continuity/Disaster Recovery controls, Subcontractor reviews.
Conduct On-Site Visits with the supplier – virtually.
Review /recommend IT contract clauses.
Assist in the development, maintenance, and implementation of information risk policies and procedures as well as the monitoring processes and measures to enforce those policies.
Research and investigate independently new issues and innovations to maintain currency of technical expertise.

How will you create impact?

By demonstrating strong risk governance and oversight through the appropriate design, implementation and/or execution of the Enterprise Risk Management framework for the business on a global basis. The individual will work to ensure a consistent and integrated approach is applied to risk reporting while ensuring the risks of the major business segments are well covered.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage, and act for their benefit.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
3-5 years of Third-Party Risk Assessment work experience.
2-3 years IT auditing or equivalent experience.
CISA, CISSP certifications are preferred.
CRISC is preferred.
Critical thinking and have excellent organization and planning abilities.
Ability to balance competing demands with little management direction/support.
Excellent communication, presentation, negotiation and influencing skills.
A passion for risk and a positive attitude.

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Values-first culture: We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.

Continuous innovation: We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.

item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time

JR24100739
The Opportunity
This Fund Propositions Sr. Manager is accountable for the overall Fund Propositions Strategy and Fund portfolio management journey for new and existing customers. Lead new fund launches, enhancement, and rationalization of existing fund range, in order to support savings & investment product propositions as well as achievement of the Company’s strategic targets
Responsibilities
Develop and manage the fund propositions portfolio, including launching new funds, recommending new fund strategies, and enhancing or rationalizing the existing fund range.
Drive segment-based, customer-centric fund propositions to address different customer goals, needs, and investment risk profiles.
Propose fund and portfolio solutions to grow the savings and investments categories through partnering with Manulife Investment Management and Trust Corporation.
Develop the overall fund portfolio strategy for single premium and regular premium variable life products, including managing the customer journey from solution/fund offer to monitoring.
Recommend fund allocation or portfolio management strategies for specific target segments, for both new and existing customers.
Develop the investment and fund education and content program for distributors and customers to drive better transparency of fund performance, enhance customer and distributor understanding of funds and investment-linked offerings, and improve product/fund suitability to customer profiles.
Accountable for the implementation of fund development and fund review governance by acting as the secretariat and driving content for the Unit-Linked Fund Committee, in collaboration with MIMTC.
Regularly review fund performance to ensure the competitiveness of fund propositions and returns compared to peer funds.
Regularly review fund management fees with the Pricing team to align with financial objectives.
Work collaboratively with channels and bancassurance partners to drive channel-specific fund propositions and develop various sales enablers, including fund playbooks and tools applicable to different partners and segments.
Work with the Product Implementation team to ensure that product/fund or fund management features can be delivered as planned.
Closely monitor updates and changes in financial markets, including but not limited to industry trends, competitor movements, market opportunities and threats, and specific client segments, to identify potential business opportunities and adjust and improve fund positioning accordingly.
Act as a resource speaker for distributor and customer events.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.
What we are looking for
At least 5 years’ relevant experience in life insurance experience or fund development/ asset management, product development and management. Experience in trust, fund analysis, fund management or fund distribution strategy an advantage.
Experience in a strategy-oriented role, or product line/business line ownership role is a must
Possess in-depth understanding of insurance and investment products, financial need analysis, sales process and industry developments
Excellent knowledge of retail financial markets and wealth management products.
Excellent public communication skills with relevant experience in conducting large-audience forums/talks on financial planning and investment topics
Excellent stakeholder management, facilitation and presentation skills
Strong drive and proven ability to manage multiple initiatives in parallel and make optimal use of resources
Highly organized and detail-oriented
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.
Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.

item.title: Manulife: Head of Brand, Content, Design & Propositions, Japan
item.description:
locations
Tokyo
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: November 30, 2024 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24101034
Manulife Life Insurance Co., Ltd. is part of the Manulife Group headquartered in Canada and is a life insurance company that specializes in wealth management solutions, offering various retirement and asset building products and services that address customer concerns around longevity preparedness and legacy provision.
Head of Brand, Content, Design & Propositions reports into the Chief Marketing Officer. This position requires strategic thinking, executive stakeholder management and creative campaign execution excellence. The Brand, Content, Design & Propositions team focuses on 1) brand and marketing, 2) content development and UIUX design, and 3) product proposition development.
This position is responsible for contributing to Manulife JP’s achievement of the company’s must-win priorities by significantly upscaling our company’s ability to attract new customers through brand and product propositions that build and develop a long-term loyal customer base for Manulife Japan.
The position oversees the proposal and delivery of marketing initiatives in line with our localised global brand strategy; creates product propositions tailored to key customer and distributor segments and owns the full customer lifecycle communications from acquisition, engagement to cross-sell/up-sell with advanced analytics, personalized content, and marketing automation.
We’re looking for someone who sees an opportunity to innovate insurance marketing and has the drive and talent to become a digital, customer-centric leader in the industry.
Responsibilities:
Reporting to the Chief Marketing Officer Japan, the Head of Brand, Content, Design & Propositions JP will design and deliver the overall Brand, Content, Design and Propositions strategy to help the company achieve the must-win priorities with a specific focus on the brand awareness to boost sales.
Branding:
Develop, lead and execute a specific and measurable brand proposition that accurately targets different segments through research and knowledge such as geographical spending power, spending preferences & behaviours, digital savviness in order to create brand awareness and interest in Manulife products ahead of market competitors.
Establish and deliver initiatives that amplify the brand and associated brand KPIs to support the growth of the business in Japan. This will involve working with appointed creative and media agencies to successfully deliver on campaign calendars.
Manage brand governance across Manulife Japan to ensure adherence to company and regulatory standards and amplification of all external assets.
Identify sponsorship opportunities that underpin Manulife Japan’s brand and customer value propositions and ensure alignment to our Impact Agenda, brand strategy and business objectives. Ensure measurable return on investment through associated marketing and sponsorship efforts.
Advise and support Manulife Japan’s bancassurance and independent advisory partners on multiple brand initiatives to help drive and meet sales growth.
Partner closely with regional (potentially global) and local stakeholders on initiative co-creation and delivery.
Propositions:
Build a robust propositions capability which helps bring the company’s solutions to life through customer and distributor facing assets through seamless collaboration within the Marketing function and across other departments.
Content & Design:
Create and activate Manulife Japan’s content strategy that brings the Manulife Japan brand to life and drives engagement through the funnel, with the objective of improving acquisition, engagement, up-sell and cross-sell.
Drive a robust approach to the creation and delivery of content across our platforms, including our agency force.
Work in partnership with Growth Marketing Team to optimize Manulife’s brand digital traffic across multiple communication channels for customers and the company’s agency force and distribution channel partners.
Drive the adoption of our design standards across all UX/UI, utilising in house talent and an approved design roster.
Overall:
Drive continued thought leadership on Brand, Content, Design and Propositions across the company as the established “go-to” subject matter expert.
Overall budget and KPI management for the above responsibilities including management of various agency partners.
Lead and cultivate a high-performing team by proving guidance and support on team members’ professional and career development opportunities to stay on top of the most advanced marketing and brand digital innovation.
Knowledge/Skills/Competencies/Education:
A result-driven professional with a university degree and minimum 15 years of experience in marketing & branding (of which at least 5 years in a senior leadership role) with experience in evaluating and implementing initiatives to meet the Manulife Japan’s strategic objectives.
Proven experience and track record in digital branding innovation that reflected in sales growth.
Proven experience in building and leading cross-collaboration marketing/branding/communication projects across departments.
Experience and track record in successful Branding, Advertising and Product Marketing initiatives that proved to be relevant, memorable, and distinguished from competitors.
Comfortable with all areas of the marketing mix including brand strategy, creative, production, media planning and buying, performance marketing, research, sponsorship, content.
Master-level understanding and experience with ‘Digital’ and data driven design.
Master-level understanding of statistical methods to facilitate targeting, segmentation, revenue generation, retention and marketing campaigns.
Extensive experience of successfully managing and delivering results with third party vendors, shared-services, brand & content delivery practice, COE or customer consulting.
Experience and proficiency in working with Agile methods.
Customer oriented.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
